{{Quote|My men will not be called cowards, my Lord! They fought the traitorous King Lahzar to get here, they stormed the Sith blockade in orbit—|Valenthyne Farfalla to Hoth|<ref name="JvS" />}}
'''Lahzar''' was a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[King/Legends|king]] who lived during the [[New Sith Wars]] between the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order's]] [[Army of Light/Legends|Army of Light]] and the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Brotherhood of Darkness]].<ref name="TEA" /> The [[Gender/Legends|king]] was a treacherous tyrant and,<ref name="Encyclopedia" /> around [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]],<ref name="Date">''[[Darth Bane: Path of Destruction]]'' establishes that [[Valenthyne Farfalla]]'s arrival on [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]] before the [[Seventh Battle of Ruusan|final battle]] between the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] there, which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' placed in [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]]. Therefore, Lahzar's betrayal of the Jedi must have taken place around that [[Time/Legends|time]].</ref> he betrayed the Jedi and revealed his true nature by attacking the [[Farfalla's Knights|forces]] of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Valenthyne Farfalla]],<ref name="Encyclopedia" /> but his [[Navy/Legends|navy]] was defeated by Farfalla in the [[Tholatin system/Legends|Tholatin system]].<ref name="TEA" /> Lahzar's actions delayed the arrival of the Jedi Master's reinforcements<ref name="Encyclopedia" /> to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="TEA" /> [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]], which allowed the Sith to begin attacking a Jedi supply caravan.<ref name="Darth Bane">''[[Darth Bane: Path of Destruction]]''</ref> When Farfalla eventually arrived, the [[Jedi Lord]] [[Hoth (Jedi Lord)|Hoth]] chastised Farfalla for his delayed appearance, to which the Jedi Master responded indignantly that his men had fought Lahzar to reach Ruusan.<ref name="JvS" />

==Behind the scenes==
Lahzar was first mentioned in the [[Jedi vs. Sith 4|fourth issue]] of the comic series ''[[Star Wars: Jedi vs. Sith]]'', which was written by [[Darko Macan]] and published by [[Dark Horse Comics]]<ref name="JvS" /> on [[July 18]], [[2001]].<ref name="Marvel">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54301/star_wars_jedi_vs_sith_2001_4|text=Jedi vs. Sith 4}}</ref> Lahzar was not mentioned in the [[2006]] novel ''[[Darth Bane: Path of Destruction]]'', which adapts the events of ''Jedi vs. Sith'' with some differences, particularly the timing of Farfalla's arrival on Ruusan, therefore affecting the chronology of his battle with King Lahzar.<ref name="Darth Bane" /> The comics portrayed Farfalla's arrival in the midst of a Sith [[Massacre on Ruusan|attack]] that occurred after the Sith attempted to create a [[thought bomb]].<ref name="JvS" /> In the novel, the scene where Farfalla and Hoth confront each other is portrayed as Farfalla's return to Ruusan after [[Standard month/Legends|months]] of absence, during which he was gathering Jedi reinforcements. The battle in the novel is instead portrayed as an attack on a supply caravan under Hoth's command, with the attack following the attempt to create the thought bomb occurring later.<ref name="Darth Bane" /> This article follows the novel's portrayal of events.
